mission.title: keda: Provide SQL Server scaler
mission.description: Provide SQL Server scaler. Requested by 8+ users.

steps.title: Check current keda deployment
steps.description:
Verify your keda version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
helm list -n keda 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working keda installation.

steps.title: Review keda configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant keda configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
kubectl get configmap -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=keda
```
Provide SQL Server scaler that is able to work with SQL Server & Azure SQL Database.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Provide SQL Server scaler
steps.description:
This PR adds a built-in scaler that uses a Microsoft SQL Server (MSSQL) database as the event source. It's very similar to both the existing mysql and postgresql scalers and is intended to work with both self-hosted SQL Server databases and SQL Server databases hosted in managed clouds.

Documentation PR: https://github.com/kedacore/keda-docs/pull/367

There is still more work to do on this PR, which is tracked in the checklist below. This also includes end-to-end testing. However, I wanted to

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
kubectl get events -n keda --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Provide SQL Server scaler" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: This PR adds a built-in scaler that uses a Microsoft SQL Server (MSSQL) database as the event source. It's very similar to both the existing mysql and postgresql scalers and is intended to work with both self-hosted SQL Server databases and SQL Server databases hosted in managed clouds.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with keda installed or the issue environment reproducible.
